doctor_description: Coronary artery calcification with PCP referral recommendation
validator_description: Coronary artery calcification with PCP referral recommendation
management_impact: None — both reports document CAC with PCP referral
No management change — incidental valve calcification not clinically actionable on chest CT alone
notes: Doctor documents tricuspid valve calcification not confirmed by validator. Minor overcall with no management impact.

doctor_description: T11 vertebral body height loss noted
management_impact: No management change — incidental finding not confirmed by validator; no actionable consequence
notes: Doctor documents T11 height loss not echoed by validator. Minor characterization difference with no management impact.

management_impact: No management change — incidental finding consistent with secretions; does not alter management
notes: Doctor omits bronchial mucous contents noted by validator. Clinically insignificant omission.

management_impact: No immediate management change from CT report alone — bilateral gynecomastia is incidental; clinical correlation warranted but not urgent
notes: Doctor omits bilateral gynecomastia noted by validator. Should be documented but does not change immediate management.

validator_description: Subcapsular right renal lesion growing from 1.0 cm (2022) to 1.6 cm (Jun 2025) to 1.9 cm (Mar 2026), density 23 HU — indeterminate; CT/MR with contrast recommended
management_impact: Doctor completely misses a growing indeterminate renal lesion (1.0 → 1.6 → 1.9 cm over 4 years). Validator recommends CT/MR with contrast for further characterization. This directly changes management — contrast-enhanced imaging workup required.
notes: Critical miss: a growing subcapsular renal lesion with indeterminate density (23 HU) that has increased from 1.0 to 1.9 cm over 4 years requires further workup. Doctor's report makes no mention of this. Management change is definitive.

doctor_description: Pulmonary nodules with Fleischner follow-up recommendation stated
validator_description: Pulmonary nodules with Fleischner follow-up recommendation stated
notes: Both reports address nodules with Fleischner follow-up interval. Concordant.

management_impact: No management change — minimal 4 mm pleural effusion overcalled by doctor; validator explicitly states no pleural effusion. No workup triggered.
notes: Doctor reports 4 mm left pleural effusion; validator says none. Likely gravitational change or technical artifact. No management impact.

finding_category: Left lung volume reduction / mediastinal shift
validator_description: Reduced left lung volume with mediastinal shift toward the left
management_impact: No immediate management change — contextual finding related to fibrosing mediastinitis; does not independently trigger additional workup beyond the mediastinal disease workup
notes: Validator documents reduced left lung volume with mediastinal shift; doctor omits. Secondary to fibrosing mediastinitis characterization difference.

doctor_description: Adrenal lesion — recommends CT follow-up in 6-12 months
validator_description: Lipid-rich adrenal adenoma (-5 HU) — no imaging follow-up required per ACR White Paper
management_impact: Doctor incorrectly recommends follow-up CT for a lipid-rich adenoma (-5 HU) that meets ACR criteria for benign adenoma requiring no follow-up (Measure 405). Unnecessary imaging would be triggered.
notes: Doctor recommends 6-12 month follow-up CT; validator correctly applies Measure 405 — no follow-up needed for lipid-rich adenoma at -5 HU. MIPS violation by doctor.

validator_description: Subchondral cysts of the right shoulder joint
management_impact: No management change — incidental musculoskeletal finding with no actionable consequence from chest CT
notes: Doctor omits right shoulder subchondral cysts. Incidental finding, no management impact.

doctor_description: Mediastinal mass / soft tissue process — uncharacterized
validator_description: Fibrosing mediastinitis — calcified mediastinal mass with associated left pulmonary vein/artery encasement
management_impact: Validator provides specific diagnosis of fibrosing mediastinitis with vascular encasement; doctor leaves uncharacterized. Both describe mediastinal pathology but validator's characterization is clinically more specific. No immediate change in acute management but directs workup differently.
notes: Validator specifically diagnoses fibrosing mediastinitis with encasement of left pulmonary vessels; doctor describes mediastinal process without specific characterization. This is a meaningful characterization difference that provides clinical context.

doctor_description: Pulmonary nodules with Fleischner follow-up stated
validator_description: Pulmonary nodules with Fleischner follow-up stated
Thyroid nodule — no follow-up, explicitly stated
management_impact: None — both agree no follow-up needed
